subject: [PATCH v2 16/21] lockd, nfsd: RCU-protect nlmsvc_ops dispatch

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch addresses a use-after-free bug in the lockd and nfsd modules by RCU-protecting the nlmsvc_ops dispatch. The bug occurs when the nfsd module is removed, causing stale loads of nlmsvc_ops and resulting in either a NULL dereference or a module-text use-after-free. To fix this, the patch declares nlmsvc_ops as __rcu, publishes it via rcu_assign_pointer(), and clears it via RCU_INIT_POINTER() + synchronize_rcu(). Additionally, a struct module *owner field is added to nlmsvc_binding and the module is pinned across indirect calls with try_module_get/module_put. When the binding is torn down, fput() is used to avoid leaking struct file references.
